approximations: High-temperature expansion of the one-loop thermal potential (small field/T, valid in the supercooled regime as documented in backend).
Tree-level potential is a single quartic; classical scale invariance forbids any explicit mass term.
Gauge-boson back-reaction on the scalar quartic dominates over the negligible scalar self-loop, so the scalar gb is fixed by beta_lambda and not scanned.

gauge_groups.name: SU(2)_D
gauge_groups.notes: Confining-free perturbative dark gauge sector; fully broken by the scalar vev so no light non-abelian remnant.

global_symmetries.name: classical scale (conformal) invariance
global_symmetries.notes: No dimensionful parameter in the Lagrangian; broken radiatively via Coleman-Weinberg dimensional transmutation.

global_symmetries.name: custodial SO(4) ~ SU(2)_L x SU(2)_R of the doublet potential
global_symmetries.notes: Accidental global symmetry of a single complex doublet with a single quartic; not gauged.

particle_content.name: Phi
particle_content.notes: Complex SU(2)_D doublet, 4 real dof. After SSB: 3 Goldstones eaten, 1 radial CW pseudo-dilaton chi remains.

particle_content.name: W_D
particle_content.notes: 3 dark gauge bosons, each massive after SSB (3 polarizations) => 9 dof. Mass m_W = g*chi/2.

particle_content.name: chi
particle_content.notes: Physical radial scalar. Mass fixed by CW relation m_chi^2 = beta_lambda * chi0^2 (dependent).

lagrangian_terms.name: scalar kinetic
lagrangian_terms.notes: D_mu Phi = (d_mu - i g (sigma^a/2) W^a_mu) Phi; gauge invariant under SU(2)_D.

lagrangian_terms.name: gauge kinetic
lagrangian_terms.notes: Standard non-abelian Yang-Mills kinetic term, a=1,2,3.

lagrangian_terms.name: scalar potential
lagrangian_terms.notes: Only quartic term allowed by classical scale invariance. No mass term mu^2 Phi^dagger Phi.

free_parameters.name: g
free_parameters.determination: independent dark SU(2)_D gauge coupling; controls FOPT strength and supercooling.

free_parameters.name: chi0
free_parameters.determination: independent symmetry-breaking scale (vev, GeV); sets the GW peak frequency in the PTA band.

dependent.lambda: Quartic coupling fixed by Coleman-Weinberg minimization (renormalization-scale dependent; not used by backend, never scanned).
dependent.beta_lambda: beta_lambda = 1/(8*pi**2) * (n_W * gb_W**4 + 1 * gb_scalar**4); with gb_W = g/2, n_W = 9.
dependent.gb_scalar: gb_scalar = sqrt(beta_lambda); scalar mass/vev ratio, fixed self-consistently, negligible back-reaction.
dependent.m_chi: m_chi = sqrt(beta_lambda) * chi0; CW pseudo-dilaton mass.

ssb_pattern.notes: A single doublet vev fully breaks SU(2)_D; all 3 gauge bosons acquire mass, 3 Goldstones eaten.

vev_convention.convention: Phi = (1/sqrt(2)) * (0, chi)^T in unitary gauge along the radial direction; <chi> = chi0.
vev_convention.notes: Single real background field chi; m_W = g*chi/2.

tree_level_potential.notes: Background-field tree potential along radial direction. Pure quartic, no mass term.

field_dependent_masses.notes: Three dark gauge bosons, m_W^2 = g^2 chi^2/4 from the doublet kinetic term. gb_W = g/2.
Radial CW scalar; gb fixed self-consistently by the gauge content (gb_scalar = sqrt(beta_lambda)). Added automatically by the backend, do not double count.

thermal_masses.notes: Debye/thermal masses enter only via the high-temperature coefficient msq = T^2/12 * sum_B dof*gb^2 computed internally by the backend get_coeffs; no separate numeric input required. The backend reconstructs the thermal mass term from the gb and dof of each species.

expression_symbols.meaning: CW beta function combination = 1/(8 pi^2)(sum_B n gb^4 - sum_F n gf^4).

assumptions: Dark sector is in thermal equilibrium with the SM bath (xi = 1 in the backend), so a common temperature applies.
No fermions are introduced; radiative symmetry breaking is guaranteed since bosons dominate (beta_lambda > 0).
Perturbativity: g <= ~1.5 keeps the one-loop CW analysis reliable.
Single scalar background direction; the doublet potential admits one radial flat direction lifted by CW.

mapping.scalar_handling: boson_gbs['scalar'] and dof=1 added automatically by SemiAnalyticPipeline via get_scalar_mass().

backend_compatibility.notes: Single-field classically scale-invariant model, no tree mass term, all masses proportional to one vev. Exactly the regime of backend/semianalytic_pipeline.py. beta_lambda = (9*(g/2)^4 + gb_scalar^4)/(8 pi^2) > 0, ensuring radiative SSB and a positive CW pseudo-dilaton mass.
